rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF GLORIA ABURTO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/14/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The petition caption does not disclose that fees are to be a lien against the real property. [CRC 7.102.] (2) Did the conservator intend to amend her petition to request compensation? Paragraph 11 of the petition seems to suggest this. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/31/24. (An amendment must be filed to correct the pleading caption or to include compensation for the conservator. An amendment requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).]) Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BENJAMIN BATES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. In August 2023, the petitioner paid $2,630.50 to her attorneys out of the estate as "extraordinary fees related to recovering Hummer" without prior court approval. See Probate Code section 10501(a)(2) & CRC 7.700(a). The attorneys request the Court's ratification of the payment for the attorneys' extraordinary services, or a deduction of the unauthorized payment from the attorneys' statutory fees for ordinary services. CRC 7.700(b) provides in relevant part that: "In addition to imposing any other sanctions authorized by law against the personal representative or the attorney for the personal representative, the court may surcharge the personal representative for payment of fees for extraordinary services in advance of an order of the court authorizing their payment. The surcharge may include interest at the legal rate from the date of payment." PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF WILLIAM COLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The decedent was apparently survived by two sisters, so her third amended petition was untrue at items 6f and 8 (which indicated that he was survived by just Petitioner as "next of kin," which she is not). 2) Since siblings are second generation, the parents must be identified, and their dates of death set forth if they are deceased. [LRSF 14.18 C.] 3) The sisters have priority, so they must decline to serve or must both nominate Petitioner. 4) Notice mailed to the two sisters was of the "Amended" petition, not this third amended petition. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT A. CHASE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/14/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (2) Notice was mailed to Darcie Quinn and to the Berkeley Repertory Theatre at different addresses from the ones shown on Exhibit B to the petition. (3) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (4) The disbursements schedule shows that all of the costs (but for the "cost retainer" to the attorney) were already paid out of the estate. (5) Not all of the disbursements indicate who the payee was. [Probate Code Section 1062(b).] (6) What portion of the "cost retainer" was used? (Costs advanced by the attorney must be itemized.) Any unused portion of that "cost retainer" must be subtracted from the attorney's statutory fee. (7) If the net amount on hand as of 8/31/24 was $788,610.64, why does Exhibit J show $793,072.86? (8) General pecuniary bequests not made within one year from the date of death bear 7% interest. [Prob. C. Sect. 12003.] (9) Did the executor intentionally not include receipts (income) in the calculation of the statutory compensation? (10) A petition for final distribution must also state the date on which notice was mailed to the FTB. [LRSF 14.35 I.2.] An amendment to the petition or a fully amended petition must be filed to address these issues. Notice of the amendment (or amended petition) must be given after the amending pleading is e-filed. [CRC 7.53(a).] Please provide a revised proposed order and courtesy copies of any new filed papers to the assigned examiner by 12/31/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The first petition was filed on 8/1/23, not 4/19/21. The executor was appointed on 8/29/23, not 8/1/23.) NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate and distribute an estate must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution." [LRSF 14.35 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).]

Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (Notice mailed before a pleading is e-filed â when it becomes publicly viewable â is defective. [LRSF 14.14 A.) (2) There is no Attachment 8 attached to this second amended petition. (3) If Lenda Taylor died on 9/11/2020, she POST-deceased Doris Taylor. Was there no estate opened for her? An affidavit pursuant to Probate Code Section 13100 must be filed, if appropriate, by the person who would have been entitled to her interest. (4) Does Isaiah Taylor have any issue? Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/24/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINCOLN CHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/18/2025 per petitioner (in pro per)'s request (via e-mail on 11/14/2024). Notes were e-mailed to petitioner on 11/05/2024.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANDREW CHU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/14/25. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) Notice must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] (3) The proof of service filed on 10/28/24 was incomplete (unsigned verification). (4) There is no attachment 3f(2)(a) to the petition indicating how Petitioner is entitled to Letters. (5) Item 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. (6) What is Rachelle Chu's relationship to the decedent? (7) The petitioner, the person named in item 2 of the petition, is missing from item 8 of the petition. (8) The two minors (presumably both the issue of the decedent) must be represented by a guardian ad litem. An ex parte petition must be filed to have a guardian ad litem appointed, to whom notice must be given (or who may waive notice of the hearing of this petition). (9) Item 2 of the Acknowledgment of Receipt of the Duties and Liabilities was incomplete. (10) If the estate value is estimated to be just $87,000, why is a formal proceeding necessary? Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of the petition and any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 12/31/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
